title: Clusy pitches an agent-native notebook for ML and data science workflows
friendlyTitle: Clusy wants to make data science notebooks more agent-like
summary: Clusy presents a notebook platform where users describe an ML outcome in plain language and the system helps source data, inspect it, choose architecture and compute, then execute the workflow end to end.
friendlySummary: Clusy lets users describe a machine-learning task in normal language, then helps plan and run the steps inside a notebook.
whyItMatters: Data work is moving toward agent-managed workflows where the notebook becomes an execution surface, not just a scratchpad. If the approach works, smaller teams could prototype fine-tunes and analysis pipelines with less manual glue work.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This could make advanced data work easier for people who know the goal but do not want to manage every technical step by hand.
